name: Law 18.331 on the Protection of Personal Data and Habeas Data Action (LPDP)
summary: Uruguay's comprehensive data protection law was one of the earliest in Latin America, earning an EU adequacy determination in 2012. It establishes principles for the processing of personal data, creates a regulatory authority, and grants data subjects habeas data rights.
keyRequirements: Obtain prior, express, and informed consent for data processing
Register all databases with the URCDP
Implement appropriate technical and organizational security measures
Ensure data quality, accuracy, and relevance
Respect data subject rights (access, rectification, inclusion, suppression)
Restrict cross-border transfers to countries with adequate protection
Comply with purpose limitation and proportionality principles
penalties: Administrative sanctions include warnings, fines up to 500,000 indexed units (~USD 65,000), suspension of the database for up to 5 business days, or cancellation of the database. Enforced by the Unidad Reguladora y de Control de Datos Personales (URCDP).
